items.scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop at Ashwood Community Studio. After paying the $85 registration fee, you discovered the session has been moved from Saturday morning to Sunday afternoon, which conflicts with a family commitment you cannot reschedule.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Whitford. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the scheduling conflict caused by the change
Explain why the original Saturday time was important
Suggest possible solutions or alternative arrangements
items.subject: Conflict with new Sunday workshop time

items.scenario: You are a second-year biology major preparing for your microbiology midterm. After attending three review sessions, you remain confused about how the professor expects you to apply the experimental design framework to short-answer questions on the upcoming exam.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Lindstrom. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific concepts that remain unclear
Explain the steps you have already taken to study
Ask whether additional resources or office hours are available
items.subject: Microbiology midterm preparation question

items.scenario: You ordered a refurbished noise-canceling headset from Audiograph Electronics three weeks ago. The right earcup produces persistent crackling and the carrying case promised in the listing was missing from the shipment when it arrived at your dormitory address.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Calloway. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the defect and the missing accessory in detail
Provide the order number and relevant purchase details
Suggest a remedy such as replacement, partial refund, or repair
items.subject: Defective headset and missing case on order AG-71422

items.scenario: Your friend Alex invited you to drive together to the regional kayaking festival next weekend. You have realized that you must complete a research paper draft by Sunday evening, which means you can only attend the Saturday events and need to return earlier than originally planned.
items.direction: Write an email to Alex. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the change in your availability for the trip
Explain the academic obligation requiring an early return
Suggest workable transportation arrangements for both of you
items.subject: Festival weekend plan and early Sunday return
